challenges.title: RTL-Aware Info Card
challenges.description: Create a simple info card component that uses logical properties for padding, margin, and border. The card should have a left accent border (in LTR) that automatically moves to the right side in RTL. Use margin-inline: auto to center it.
challenges.hint: Focus on border-inline-start for the accent — it automatically swaps sides in RTL. Use padding-inline and padding-block for inside spacing.

starter_code.html:
Module Title

This card should work in both LTR and RTL without any CSS overrides.

solution.html:
Module Title

This card works in both LTR and RTL without any CSS overrides!

challenges.expected_outcome: A card centered with margin-inline: auto, with correct padding and a left accent in LTR that becomes a right accent in RTL.

challenges.title: Multi-Direction Navigation Bar
challenges.description: Build a horizontal navigation bar that works correctly in LTR and RTL. Use logical properties for spacing between nav items, the logo positioning, and the active indicator. The nav items should be evenly spaced using justify-content: end or similar logical approaches.
challenges.hint: Use margin-inline-end for spacing between nav items (adds space after each item in LTR, before each item in RTL). Use padding-inline for the nav container.

starter_code.html:
Logo
Home Products About Contact

solution.html:
Logo
Home Products About Contact

challenges.expected_outcome: A fully functional navigation bar that works correctly in both LTR and RTL, with the logo on the start side and items properly spaced.

challenges.title: International Article Layout with Grid
challenges.description: Build an article page layout that uses CSS Grid combined with logical properties. The layout should have: a sticky sidebar on the inline-start side, main content in the center, and an aside on the inline-end side. Include logical sizing, positioning, and border radii. The layout must work correctly in LTR, RTL, and vertical writing modes.
challenges.hint: Use grid-template-areas with logical-aware naming. Use inline-size instead of width, block-size instead of height. For sticky positioning, use inset-block-start. Remember that border-start-start-radius changes meaning in RTL and vertical modes.
